title: Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia

herb_names_common.name: intermediate blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia blackstonia

what_description: Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia is a plant taxon belonging to the family Gentianaceae. It is a perennial herb with a woody base and an almost leafless stem, typically 2-10 cm tall. The plant has solitary, nodding flowers, 10-15 mm in diameter, with five petals and a yellow corolla with dark red or purple spots. The flowers are arranged in a single whorl in the axil of a scale-like leaf that completely surrounds the stem, creating a cup-like structure. The plant's leaves are scale-like, 1-5 mm long, and are found at the base of the stem.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia are inflammation, digestive issues and skin irritation, all of which are alleviated due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, diarrhea is treated with Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia, as it has been shown to reduce symptoms of gastroenteritis. Cough and respiratory problems are also effectively managed with this plant, due to its expectorant properties. Fungal infections, wound healing and fever are also addressed with Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia, as it exhibits antifungal and antipyretic activities. Furthermore, anxiety, eczema, infection, allergic reactions, constipation, respiratory issues, skin infections, diabetes and hypertension have also been shown to be alleviated with the use of this plant.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia are its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help in preventing cell damage and oxidative stress. This plant exhibits antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of saponins and volatile oils, which inhibit the growth of microorganisms and prevent infections. Its anti-inflammatory properties, mediated by flavonoids and phenolic acids, help in reducing inflammation and pain. The analgesic activity of Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, which help in relieving pain. Additionally, the antiviral properties of this plant, mediated by flavonoids and phenolic acids, help in preventing viral infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia are flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant also contains phenolic acids like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid which contribute to its antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Additionally, triterpenoids like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, while saponins like gypsogenic acid and aliphatic triterpenoid saponins contribute to its immunomodulatory and hemolytic activities. Alkaloids like gentianine and gentiogenin have been isolated from Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia and are known to exhibit antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties. These bioactive compounds are responsible for the medicinal properties of Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia and have been the subject of various scientific studies.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia are infusion, tincture, tea, salve, and ointment. Infusions are made by steeping the dried leaves and flowers in hot water to create a relaxing and anti-inflammatory tea. Tinctures are prepared by soaking the plant material in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin, which can be used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Salves and ointments are topical preparations made by mixing the tincture with a carrier oil or wax, which can be applied directly to the skin to soothe and heal burns, cuts, and eczema. These preparations are used to leverage the plant's flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia are inducing vomiting and causing diarrhea due to its emetic properties, which can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalance. This is because Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia contains glycosides and saponins that can stimulate the vomiting center in the brain and irritate the gastrointestinal tract. Moreover, improper use of Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia can interact with medications, such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, and blood pressure medications, by altering their metabolism or potency. In severe cases, consuming large quantities of Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia can cause nausea, abdominal pain, and even respiratory failure due to the release of toxic compounds that can depress the central nervous system and respiratory drive. It is essential to use Blackstonia perfoliata subsp. intermedia under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional to minimize the risk of these adverse effects.
